Title: TALEN-mediated targeted mutagenesis of more than 100 COMT copies/alleles in highly polyploid sugarcane improves saccharification efficiency without compromising biomass yield

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream branch step affecting monolignol composition
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes O-methylation of 5-hydroxyconiferaldehyde and 5-hydroxyconiferyl alcohol, diverting flux toward formation of syringyl lignin monomer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem cell wall / bagasse cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides cell wall recalcitrance and structural support
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly determines monolignol composition and contributes to target polymer accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for syringyl lignin formation; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in sugarcane leaf tissue by qRT-PCR; relative transcript abundance varied among mutant lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Top visible dewlap leaf; mutant lines showed 26.7% to 91.4% of WT transcript, RNAi line B401 showed 41.8% of WT
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Targeted mutagenesis of COMT reduced lignin content by up to 19.7%, decreased S/G ratio, and improved saccharification efficiency by up to 43.8% without significant biomass penalty in line CB6 and most field-grown mutant lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A single TALEN pair co-mutated 107 of 109 identified COMT copies/alleles in sugarcane line CB6. 2. Field-grown COMT mutant lines showed 10.9%–19.7% lignin reduction and reduced syringyl/guaiacyl ratio relative to WT. 3. COMT mutation improved glucose release/saccharification efficiency by up to 43.8%–44% from lignocellulosic biomass. 4. Biomass yield and major agronomic traits were generally not significantly different from WT, especially for line CB6.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TALEN-mediated targeted mutagenesis; Sanger sequencing of long cloned amplicons; capillary electrophoresis mutation profiling; qRT-PCR; lignin quantification; lignin monomer analysis; saccharification assay; replicated field trial
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Replicated field conditions in Citra, Florida, USA; dilute-acid pretreatment followed by enzymatic hydrolysis for saccharification assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for genome editing to improve biomass deconstruction and second-generation biofuel conversion in sugarcane; useful for multiplex co-editing in highly polyploid crops; favorable because high-level editing improved saccharification without major biomass yield loss in elite background
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Some lines showed reduced soluble solids (Brix); one Agrobacterium/indirect embryogenesis-derived line (CA17) had reduced biomass, though this may reflect somaclonal variation rather than COMT effect; disease resistance and lodging were not significantly altered in this trial
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study focused on >100 COMT copies/alleles in a highly polyploid genome. Lignin reduction did not correlate linearly with measured mutation frequency or transcript level, suggesting many copies/alleles may be nonfunctional, weakly expressed, or differ in mutation consequence. Brown coloration after crushing/cutting was associated with lignin modification.
